steps.title: Identify the contrast the blank must complete
steps.content:
The sentence says Greek and Roman sculpture is “sometimes characterized as idealizing human subjects, but in fact” many sculptures depict people in “decidedly ______ ways.” The phrase “but in fact” signals a direct contrast, so the blank must mean the opposite of idealizing — showing subjects as less than perfect.
  • Choice A — implausible: Means unrealistic or hard to believe; idealized art is also unrealistic, so this is not an opposite of idealizing. ✗
  • Choice B — unflattering: Means showing a subject in an unattractive or unfavorable light — the direct opposite of idealizing. ✓
  • Choice C — superficial: Means shallow or lacking depth; this does not contrast with idealization. ✗
  • Choice D — imprecise: Means not exact or accurate; this does not oppose the idea of presenting subjects as ideal. ✗

steps.title: Confirm and conclude
steps.content: “Unflattering” is the only word that is the direct antonym of “idealizing,” completing the contrast set up by “but in fact.” That is the correct answer.
